changed_functions.name: SymCryptMlDsaSampleInBall (sub_18002A2E8)
key_changes: Added iteration counter limit (0x79/121 iterations) to the inner rejection-sampling loop that was previously unbounded
Function now returns error code 0x8008 (SYMCRYPT_EXTERNAL_FAILURE) on loop overflow instead of looping
Patched version pushes r12 register to hold the counter variable
changed_functions.security_notes: This is SymCrypt's ML-DSA (FIPS 204) SampleInBall routine, which samples the challenge polynomial c. The unpatched version has an unbounded inner rejection loop: `do { SymCryptKeccakExtract(&var_138, &var_148, 1); } while (var_148[0] > rbx_1);` where the byte squeezed from the SHAKE/Keccak sponge is compared to an index threshold; if it stays above the threshold the loop does not terminate. With a well-formed sponge this terminates with overwhelming probability, so the pre-patch risk is non-termination only under a pathological/corrupted sponge state (CWE-835), not an attacker-controlled ASN.1 path. The patched version adds `if (rdi_1 >= 0x79) { r14 = 0x8008; goto label_180027512; }` to cap iterations. Called from SymCryptMlDsaSignEx (sub_180020c7c) during ML-DSA signing.

changed_functions.name: SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly (sub_18002A06C)
key_changes: Added iteration counter (rsi) with limit check `cmp esi, 0x1e1` to the previously unbounded sponge-byte rejection loop
Function now returns 0x8008 (SYMCRYPT_EXTERNAL_FAILURE) when the loop exceeds 0x1e1 (481) sponge bytes instead of continuing indefinitely
Added push r15 to hold the first argument (rcx) since r14 is now repurposed for the output buffer pointer
changed_functions.security_notes: This is SymCrypt's ML-DSA (FIPS 204) RejBoundedPoly routine, which rejection-samples the coefficients of a secret polynomial in [-eta, eta] by squeezing bytes from a SHAKE/Keccak sponge via SymCryptKeccakExtract (sub_18002bb78). The outer loop is bounded by the coefficient count (0x100), but out-of-range nibbles decode to a 0x80 skip marker that does not advance the count, and the unpatched version has no independent limit on how many sponge bytes are consumed. A long run of skip-producing nibbles keeps the loop reading (CWE-835 non-termination); the byte source is fresh sponge output, so there is no fixed input buffer to overread. The patched version adds `if (rsi >= 0x1e1) { rdi = 0x8008; break; }`. Called from SymCryptMlDsaExpandS (sub_180029484) during ML-DSA secret-vector expansion.

changed_functions.name: SymCryptMlDsaExpandS (sub_180029484)
key_changes: Return value from SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly (sub_18002a06c / sub_180027128) is now checked — previously it was discarded (called as void)
Patched version captures the status (mov edi, eax; test eax, eax; jnz loc_1800265FA) and exits immediately if any per-polynomial sampling fails
Function now returns the actual error status instead of always returning the result of SymCryptWipeAsm cleanup (0)
changed_functions.security_notes: This is SymCrypt's ML-DSA (FIPS 204) ExpandS routine, which expands the secret vectors s1/s2 by calling SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly (sub_18002a06c) once per polynomial. In the unpatched version each call is invoked as void — its return is discarded — and the function always returns the result of SymCryptWipeAsm (sub_180011800), which is 0. If RejBoundedPoly fails (e.g. hits its sampling bound and returns 0x8008 SYMCRYPT_EXTERNAL_FAILURE), the failure is lost and expansion reports success with an incompletely sampled secret vector (CWE-252/CWE-754). The patched version captures the return: `v9 = SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly(...); if (v9) break;` and returns v9. This runs during ML-DSA key generation / import / self-test, not Authenticode PE verification.

changed_functions.name: SymCryptRsakeyCalculatePrivateFields (sub_180002D3C)
key_changes: Added new 7th parameter (a7); an out-of-range a7 (anything but bit 0x200) now returns 0x800e

New consistency check: `if ((a7 & 0x200) != 0) return v16; if (v21 == 2) { SymCryptFdefIntMulMixedSize(prime0, prime1, a4); if (SymCryptFdefIntIsEqual(a4, modulus) != 0) return v16; } return 32782;` — this block is absent in the unpatched version
The new check multiplies the two RSA primes and compares the product to the stored modulus
changed_functions.security_notes: This is SymCrypt's SymCryptRsakeyCalculatePrivateFields, which derives the RSA private CRT fields. The unpatched version never checks that the supplied primes are consistent with the modulus. When a7 does NOT have bit 0x200 set and there are exactly 2 primes (v21==2), the patched version multiplies the two primes via SymCryptFdefIntMulMixedSize (sub_1800260e0) and compares the product to the modulus via SymCryptFdefIntIsEqual (sub_180025e1c); on mismatch it falls through to return 32782 (0x800e SYMCRYPT_INVALID_ARGUMENT). Without it, an RSA key whose primes do not multiply back to its modulus is accepted (CWE-754 missing key-material validation). Called from SymCryptRsakeySetValueInternal (sub_18001da98), reached from SymCryptRsakeySetValue (sub_18000e754) / SymCryptRsaSelftest (sub_18000e7c4). Not certificate issuer verification; 0x800e is a SymCrypt error, not TRUST_E_CERT_SIGNATURE.

changed_functions.name: SymCryptMlDsaSignEx (sub_180020C7C / patched sub_180021394)
key_changes: Added main-loop iteration bound (`mov ecx, 32Eh` at 0x180021561, i.e. s * 0x32E) with check `if (v22 >= v41) goto LABEL_16;` to cap the signing rejection loop; absent in the unpatched build
Added error return checking on the SymCryptMlDsaSampleInBall (sub_18002a2e8 / patched sub_1800273e0) call result, propagating 0x8008 on overflow (mov r15d, 8008h at 0x180021807)
changed_functions.security_notes: This is SymCrypt's ML-DSA SignEx routine (patched at 0x180021394). The unpatched version has no upper iteration limit on the main signing rejection loop and ignores the SymCryptMlDsaSampleInBall status. The patched version adds a finite iteration bound and propagates the sampler error (0x8008). Same non-termination/error-propagation hardening class as Findings 1/3/4. Called from SymCryptMlDsaSelftest (sub_180022048).

changed_functions.name: wil_details_EvaluateFeatureDependencies_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState (sub_1800576AC)
key_changes: Added new check `if (rax_1 != 0xc00) rbp_1 = 0;` to validate that both bit 10 and bit 11 are set in the feature flags
Added new post-evaluation logic: `if (rbp_1 && !rdi) rdx_2 &= 0xfffffbff;` — clears bit 10 when the flag check (rbp_1) is active but the dependency evaluation (rdi) fails
Changed the initial condition from only checking bit 6 to checking both (arg2 & 0xc00) and bit 6
changed_functions.security_notes: This is a WIL (Windows Implementation Library) feature-flag routine, not certificate policy. The unpatched version only checks bit 6 (`rdi_2 = arg2 >> 6 & 1`) to decide whether to evaluate feature dependencies. The patched version requires both bits 10 and 11 (`rax_1 = arg2 & 0xc00; if (rax_1 != 0xc00) rbp_1 = 0;`) before activating the dependency-evaluation path, and clears bit 10 on failure.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_GetCurrentFeatureEnabledState (sub_180019264)
key_changes: Restructured feature-flag computation with new condition `(rbx_3 & 0xc00) != 0xc00`
Added call to `wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled (sub_180019064)(arg1)` when the flag bits are set, which was previously a manual inline loop
New logic: `if (r14 && !rcx_6) rax_8 &= 0xfffffbff;` — clears bit 10 on dependency-check failure
changed_functions.security_notes: This is a WIL feature-flag routine, not certificate policy. The unpatched version evaluates flags using an inline loop that checks `*(rcx_7 + 0x1e)` and `*(rcx_7 + 0x1d)` directly, without the 0xc00 bit check. The patched version checks both bits 10+11 together and calls the dedicated wil_details_AreDependenciesEnabled (sub_180019064). This affects feature-flag/dependency evaluation, not signature validation.

changed_functions.name: SymCryptMlDsaKeyGenerateEx (sub_180020A14)
key_changes: Added missing error checking on return value of SymCryptMlDsaExpandS (sub_18002651c / formerly sub_180029484) — now breaks/stops if expansion fails
Changed from ignoring return value to `rbx = rax_6; if (!rax_6) { ... } else { ... }` pattern
changed_functions.security_notes: This is SymCrypt's ML-DSA KeyGenerateEx routine. The unpatched version calls SymCryptMlDsaExpandS (sub_180029484) and ignores its return value, proceeding regardless of whether secret-vector expansion succeeded. The patched version captures and checks the return value, halting on failure. This compounds with the unchecked-return bug inside SymCryptMlDsaExpandS itself (which also ignores SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly returns).

changed_functions.name: SymCryptMlDsaExpandA (sub_1800292E4)
key_changes: Added error return checking on SymCryptMlDsaRejNttPoly (sub_1800272ec / formerly sub_18002a20c) calls — if any returns non-zero, immediately exits with that error
Changed from overwriting the loop variable to propagating the error return value
changed_functions.security_notes: This is SymCrypt's ML-DSA ExpandA routine, which fills the public matrix A. The unpatched version iterates calling SymCryptMlDsaRejNttPoly (sub_18002a20c) per entry, overwriting the return value into 'result' but never checking for errors — the loop continues regardless. The patched version captures the return into rdx and, if non-zero, jumps to exit with the error (CWE-252/CWE-754).

changed_functions.name: SymCryptMlDsaPkDecode (sub_180029840)
key_changes: Added error checking on the SymCryptMlDsaExpandA (sub_180026374) return value before calling SymCryptKeccakExtract (sub_18002c284) with the result as a size parameter
Patched version: `int32_t rax_5 = SymCryptMlDsaExpandA(...); if (!rax_5) SymCryptKeccakExtract(&rax_1[2], arg4 + 0x76, rax_5 + 0x40, 1);` — only proceeds to write if no error
changed_functions.security_notes: This is SymCrypt's ML-DSA PkDecode routine. The unpatched version calls SymCryptMlDsaExpandA (sub_1800292e4) and passes its return value (potentially an error code) as a size parameter to SymCryptKeccakExtract (sub_18002bb78) without checking. If ExpandA returns an error, this could lead to an incorrect size calculation in the subsequent operation. The patched version checks the return value first.

changed_functions.name: CipProcessSIPolicyLogs (sub_1800DDF24)
key_changes: Changed global variable references (data_180046948→data_180048a00, data_1800474b8→data_1800495b0, etc.) — likely due to data layout changes
Changed loop iteration from `while (i < 4)` with `i * 0xa0` offset to `while (rsi_1 < 4)` with `rsi_1 * 0x28` offset — struct field stride changed
Added new condition at end: `if (result && arg1[0x2fc] & 0x40000 && (arg1[0x26a] < 0 || arg1[0x26b])) return sub_1800656d8(...)`

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureStateCache_ReevaluateCachedFeatureEnabledState (sub_180019100)
key_changes: Added `result = rax_3 | 0x40000;` to set bit 18 in the result flags — this bit is now always set in the WIL feature-state result
Minor register reallocation

changed_functions.name: SymCryptFdefDecideModulusType (sub_18000E99C)
key_changes: Minor register reallocation and variable renaming — no behavioral change

changed_functions.name: SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed (@0x1800706C4 -> @0x1800764A8)
key_changes: Added lock/enable gate: the decision now returns false unless the new byte cs:byte_180048F08 is set (cmp cs:byte_180048F08, r9b at 0x1800764BD). This byte has zero references in the unpatched binary and is written only by the new SIPolicySetCustomKernelSignersLockedEnabled (@0x180076558), called from a registry-backed init path (call at 0x180018EAD after ZwSetValueKey).

Added base-policy restriction: the per-policy loop now calls SIPolicyIsBasePolicy (@0x180075158, call at 0x1800764E4) and stops on any non-base policy, so supplemental policies are no longer considered. The unpatched SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed (@0x1800706C4) never called SIPolicyIsBasePolicy and scanned every loaded SI policy.

Delegated the +0x708 flag test to the new SIPolicyCheckCustomKernelSignersAllowed (@0x18007652C, call at 0x1800764F6), which re-checks the same lock byte. This helper has no content match in the unpatched binary.

Net effect: unpatched accepted the custom-kernel-signers enable bit at policy field +0x708 from ANY loaded policy (including supplemental) with no lock; patched requires an explicit lock/enable state plus a base policy. Strictly stricter.
changed_functions.security_notes: SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed decides whether a Code Integrity driver-signing evaluation may accept custom (non-Microsoft) kernel signers; its result is folded into g_CiDeveloperMode bit 0xD during SI-policy init (caller at 0x18001A4D8 unpatched / 0x18001A7AC patched, plus a second patched site at 0x18006BC0C). The unpatched version (@0x1800706C4) iterates g_SiPolicyHandles[0..g_NumberOfSiPolicies] and returns true if ANY policy has the enable bit set at +0x708 (test al,0x18 / test al,4), with no lock gate and no base/supplemental distinction, so a supplemental SI policy could enable custom kernel signers. The patched version (@0x1800764A8) refuses unless the new lock byte byte_180048F08 is set and restricts the enabling policy to a base policy via SIPolicyIsBasePolicy. This is a protection-mechanism-failure hardening (CWE-693) on the kernel-mode driver-signature-enforcement trust boundary, not part of the statically-linked SymCrypt library. Not gated by any WIL/Feature_* staging flag, and the old permissive scan is not retained. Practical abuse requires the ability to load a supplemental SI policy (a privileged operation), hence Medium severity.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
; UNPATCHED SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed @ 0x1800706C4 (scans ALL policies, no lock, no base filter)
00000001800706CA  cmp     r9d, cs:g_NumberOfSiPolicies
00000001800706D1  jnb     short loc_1800706FB
00000001800706DD  mov     rax, [rax+rdx*8]
00000001800706E1  mov     eax, [rax+708h]
00000001800706E7  test    cl, cl
00000001800706EB  test    al, 18h
00000001800706EF  test    al, 4
00000001800706F1  jnz     short loc_1800706F8
00000001800706F3  inc     r9d
00000001800706F6  jmp     short loc_1800706CA
00000001800706F8  mov     r8b, 1
; PATCHED SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed @ 0x1800764A8 (lock gate + base-policy filter + delegated flag test)
00000001800764BD  cmp     cs:byte_180048F08, r9b
00000001800764C4  jnz     short loc_1800764CA
00000001800764C6  xor     al, al
00000001800764C8  jmp     short loc_180076513
00000001800764DE  mov     rdi, [r11]
00000001800764E1  mov     rcx, rdi
00000001800764E4  call    SIPolicyIsBasePolicy
00000001800764E9  test    al, al
00000001800764EB  jz      short loc_180076510
00000001800764ED  mov     ecx, [rdi+708h]
00000001800764F3  mov     dl, sil
00000001800764F6  call    SIPolicyCheckCustomKernelSignersAllowed
; NEW SIPolicyCheckCustomKernelSignersAllowed @ 0x18007652C (re-checks lock byte)
000000018007652C  cmp     cs:byte_180048F08, 0
0000000180076533  jz      short loc_18007654C
0000000180076539  test    cl, 8
; NEW SIPolicySetCustomKernelSignersLockedEnabled @ 0x180076558 (only writer of lock byte)
0000000180076558  mov     cs:byte_180048F08, cl
000000018007655E  retn
; PATCHED registry-backed writer of the lock value
0000000180018EAD  call    SIPolicySetCustomKernelSignersLockedEnabled

security_findings.entry_point: ML-DSA (FIPS 204) key generation / key import / self-test inside SymCrypt (statically linked into ci.dll)
security_findings.description: SymCryptMlDsaExpandS expands the ML-DSA secret vectors s1 and s2 by calling SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly (sub_18002a06c) once per polynomial. In the unpatched version the call is invoked as void — its return value is discarded — and the function always returns the result of SymCryptWipeAsm (sub_180011800), which is 0. If a per-polynomial sampling call fails (for example by hitting its sampling bound and returning 0x8008 SYMCRYPT_EXTERNAL_FAILURE), that failure is lost and expansion reports success with an incompletely sampled secret vector. This is a robustness/error-propagation defect in post-quantum key handling, not an Authenticode PE signature bypass.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Reach the ML-DSA key-generation or key-import path in the unpatched ci.dll and cause SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly to return non-zero for one polynomial. The self-test path uses fixed vectors, so this is primarily a robustness concern rather than an attacker-controlled input path. In the unpatched build the failing status is dropped and SymCryptMlDsaExpandS returns 0.
security_findings.primitive: Error-propagation defect — a failed secret-polynomial sampling is masked, so ML-DSA key material may be finalized in an incompletely sampled state. Not a code-signing bypass.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED SymCryptMlDsaExpandS @ 0x180029484
; s1 loop - return value of SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly discarded:
0000000180029505  call    SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly
000000018002950A  inc     di                 ; advance index; rax (status) never read
0000000180029510  cmp     eax, r14d
0000000180029513  jb      short loc_1800294E9 ; loop regardless of status
; s2 loop at loc_180029519 identical (call at 0x180029539)
0000000180029552  call    SymCryptWipeAsm    ; cleanup; sampling status never returned
0000000180029575  retn

; PATCHED SymCryptMlDsaExpandS @ 0x18002651C
00000001800265A7  call    SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly
00000001800265AC  mov     edi, eax           ; capture status
00000001800265AE  test    eax, eax
00000001800265B0  jnz     short loc_1800265FA ; break out on error
00000001800265B2  inc     bx
00000001800265B8  cmp     ecx, r14d
00000001800265BB  jb      short loc_18002658B
0000000180026609  mov     eax, edi           ; return captured status

breakpoints.why: Break here to observe the ML-DSA secret-vector expansion loop. Watch how the return value from SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly (called inside) is discarded. The index is in di/bx; r14d holds the s1 polynomial count and ebp the s2 count (loop bounds). The function should exit early on error but does not.
Per-polynomial SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly sampler. Check rax on return — non-zero (0x8008 SYMCRYPT_EXTERNAL_FAILURE) indicates a sampling failure that is silently ignored by the caller.

watch: r14d = s1 polynomial count (*(arg1+0x15)); ebp = s2 count (*(arg1+0x14)) — loop bounds
eax after call to sub_18002a06c = status code (non-zero = failure, but ignored in unpatched)
key_offsets: 0x180029505: the call to SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly inside the s1 loop — return value discarded
0x180029539: the call to SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly inside the s2 loop — also missing error check
0x180029552: call to SymCryptWipeAsm cleanup; the sampling status is not moved into eax, so it is never returned
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Reach the ML-DSA key-generation / key-import path in the unpatched ci.dll. 2. Force SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly to return non-zero for one polynomial (e.g. its sampling-bound overflow). 3. Observe that the failing status is dropped by SymCryptMlDsaExpandS.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In the unpatched binary: SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly returns non-zero (0x8008 SYMCRYPT_EXTERNAL_FAILURE) but SymCryptMlDsaExpandS returns 0. No error propagates. The patched build returns the error.
debugging_aids.notes: The struct at arg1 has key offsets: arg1+0x14 (s2 polynomial count), arg1+0x15 (s1 polynomial count). The output uses 0x400 (1<<10) byte stride per polynomial, accessed at (rdi << 0xa) + 8 + arg4. This is ML-DSA key expansion, not a certificate array.

security_findings.entry_point: RSA key import / private-field computation inside SymCrypt (statically linked into ci.dll)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: CWE-754 - missing validation of cryptographic key material
security_findings.description: SymCryptRsakeyCalculatePrivateFields derives the RSA private CRT fields. The unpatched version never checks that the supplied prime factors are consistent with the modulus. The patched version adds a 7th parameter (a7) and, at the end of the two-prime path, when bit 0x200 is NOT set in a7 and there are exactly 2 primes (v21 == 2), multiplies the two primes with SymCryptFdefIntMulMixedSize (sub_1800260e0) and compares the product to the stored modulus with SymCryptFdefIntIsEqual (sub_180025e1c). On match it returns the running status; otherwise it falls through to return 32782 (0x800e SYMCRYPT_INVALID_ARGUMENT). The unpatched version accepts an RSA key whose primes do not multiply back to its modulus. This is RSA key validation, not certificate issuer verification, and 0x800e is a SymCrypt error, not TRUST_E_CERT_SIGNATURE.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Import an RSA key via SymCryptRsakeySetValueInternal with two primes that do not multiply to the supplied modulus, with the flags argument (a7) not having bit 0x200 set. The unpatched version derives private fields and returns success; the patched version returns 0x800e.
security_findings.primitive: Key-validation hardening — rejects an inconsistent RSA key (primes not matching the modulus) that the unpatched build silently accepts. Not a code-signing bypass.

breakpoints.why: Break at function entry. Observe arg1 (RSA key struct), a7 (flags parameter — check bit 0x200). In the unpatched version, observe that no p*q==modulus check occurs after the private-field loops.
SymCryptRsakeySetValueInternal, the caller. Check what value is passed as the 7th argument (a7) — in the unpatched version this parameter does not exist, so the stack layout is different.

watch: arg1+0x1c = number of prime factors (*(a1+28), should be 2 for the checked path)
arg1+0x78/0x80/0x88 = modulus / prime[0] / prime[1] object pointers (integer field at +0x60)
return value = 32782 (0x800e) on consistency-check failure in patched, but the running status (0) in unpatched
key_offsets: 0x180002E71: private-field loop — the new check is inserted after this in the patched version
0x180002F05: CRT inner loop — in the patched version the p*q==n check follows after this
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Import an RSA key via SymCryptRsakeySetValueInternal with two primes that do not multiply to the modulus. 2. Ensure the flags argument (a7) does not have bit 0x200 set. 3. Compute private fields. The unpatched build accepts it; the patched build returns 0x800e.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Unpatched: inconsistent key accepted (returns success). Patched: SymCryptFdefIntIsEqual (sub_180025e1c) reports non-equal, function returns 32782 (0x800e SYMCRYPT_INVALID_ARGUMENT).
debugging_aids.notes: The new a7 parameter is at position 7 (stack-based in x64 calling convention). The check applies to 2-prime RSA keys where a7 bit 0x200 is clear. SymCryptFdefIntMulMixedSize (sub_1800260e0) multiplies the primes and SymCryptFdefIntIsEqual (sub_180025e1c) compares against the modulus.

security_findings.entry_point: ML-DSA (FIPS 204) signing / challenge-polynomial sampling inside SymCrypt (statically linked into ci.dll)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: CWE-835 - loop with unreachable exit condition
security_findings.description: SymCryptMlDsaSampleInBall samples the ML-DSA challenge polynomial c (tau non-zero +/-1 coefficients) using rejection sampling. The inner loop squeezes one byte at a time from a SHAKE/Keccak sponge via SymCryptKeccakExtract (sub_18002bb78) and resamples while the byte exceeds the current index threshold (`while (var_148[0] > rbx_1)`). In the unpatched version this inner loop has no iteration limit. With a well-formed sponge it terminates with overwhelming probability, so the pre-patch risk is non-termination only under a pathological/corrupted sponge state, not attacker-controlled ASN.1. The patched version adds a counter limit of 0x79 (121) iterations, returning error 0x8008 (SYMCRYPT_EXTERNAL_FAILURE) if exceeded.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Reach SymCryptMlDsaSampleInBall via ML-DSA signing (SymCryptMlDsaSignEx). The inner loop must keep rejecting, i.e. the byte squeezed from the Keccak sponge stays above rbx_1 (which starts at 0x100 - *(arg1+0x18) and increments per accepted coefficient). Because the byte source is the sponge (not external input), this requires a pathological or corrupted sponge state rather than crafted signature bytes.
security_findings.primitive: Non-termination hardening — a defensive iteration bound on an ML-DSA rejection loop; pre-patch risk is a hang only under a corrupted sponge state.
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED SymCryptMlDsaSampleInBall @ 0x18002A2E8 - inner rejection loop:
; No iteration counter, can loop indefinitely under a corrupted sponge
000000018002A3A6  loc_18002A3A6:              ; inner loop head
000000018002A3B6  call    SymCryptKeccakExtract ; squeeze 1 byte
000000018002A3BB  movzx   eax, [rsp+160h+var_140]
000000018002A3C0  cmp     eax, ebx            ; byte vs rbx_1 threshold
000000018002A3C2  ja      short loc_18002A3A6 ; if > threshold, loop again (unbounded)

; PATCHED SymCryptMlDsaSampleInBall @ 0x1800273E0:
000000018002749B  mov     edi, r14d           ; init counter (r14d = 0)
000000018002749E  loc_18002749E:
000000018002749E  cmp     edi, 79h            ; ADDED: iteration limit (121), checked at loop head
00000001800274A1  jnb     short loc_180027502 ; ADDED: overflow -> error path
00000001800274B4  call    SymCryptKeccakExtract ; squeeze 1 byte
00000001800274BE  inc     edi                 ; ADDED: advance counter
00000001800274C0  cmp     eax, ebx
00000001800274C2  ja      short loc_18002749E ; resample (bounded by 0x79)
0000000180027502  mov     r14d, 8008h         ; ADDED: SYMCRYPT_EXTERNAL_FAILURE

breakpoints.why: Break at function entry. This is SymCryptMlDsaSampleInBall (challenge-polynomial sampling). Watch the inner loop (head loc_18002A3A6, KeccakExtract at 0x18002A3B6, ja at 0x18002A3C2) — in the unpatched version there is no counter, so the loop can spin indefinitely if squeezed sponge bytes consistently exceed rbx_1.

watch: ebx (rbx_1) = current index threshold, starts at 0x100 - *(rsi+0x18) and increments by 1 per accepted coefficient
byte [rsp+0x20] (var_148[0]) = squeezed sponge byte, compared against ebx — if always > ebx, non-terminating loop
rsi+0x18 = tau field from the ML-DSA parameter structure
r14 = output buffer pointer (arg4) where the challenge polynomial is written
key_offsets: 0x18002A398: outer loop — lea rdi, [r14+rbx*4] sets up output pointer
0x18002A3A6: inner loop head (xor r9d, r9d) — the ja resample target (UNBOUNDED in unpatched)
0x18002A3B6: call to SymCryptKeccakExtract (sub_18002bb78) to squeeze the next byte
0x18002A3C2: cmp eax, ebx / ja short loc_18002A3A6 — no counter, can spin forever
In patched: 0x18002749E has cmp edi, 79h / jnb loc_180027502
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Reach SymCryptMlDsaSampleInBall via ML-DSA signing (SymCryptMlDsaSignEx). 2. The inner loop must keep rejecting, which requires a pathological/corrupted Keccak sponge state so the squeezed byte stays above the threshold (the sponge is the byte source, not external ASN.1). 3. Observe the non-terminating inner loop.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In a debugger, the instruction pointer stays in the loop at loc_18002A3A6-0x18002A3C2, repeatedly calling SymCryptKeccakExtract at 0x18002A3B6. The call stack shows ci!SymCryptMlDsaSampleInBall called from ci!SymCryptMlDsaSignEx (sub_180020c7c). The patched build exits with 0x8008 (SYMCRYPT_EXTERNAL_FAILURE).
debugging_aids.notes: sub_18002bb78 is SymCryptKeccakExtract, which squeezes fresh bytes from a SHAKE/Keccak sponge (var_138) seeded during signing. The threshold ebx starts at 0x100 - tau and grows by 1 each accepted coefficient, so only early iterations can sustain rejection. The byte source is the sponge, so there is no external ASN.1 to craft.

security_findings.entry_point: ML-DSA (FIPS 204) key generation / secret-coefficient sampling inside SymCrypt (statically linked into ci.dll)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: CWE-835 - loop with unreachable exit condition
security_findings.description: SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly rejection-samples the coefficients of a secret polynomial in [-eta, eta] by squeezing bytes from a SHAKE/Keccak sponge via SymCryptKeccakExtract (sub_18002bb78) and mapping each nibble; out-of-range nibbles decode to a 0x80 skip marker that does not advance the coefficient count. The outer loop stops at 0x100 (256) coefficients, but the unpatched version has no independent limit on how many sponge bytes are consumed. A long run of skip-producing nibbles keeps the loop reading without filling the polynomial. Because the bytes come from the Keccak sponge (fresh output), there is no fixed input buffer to overread — the effect is non-termination, not an OOB read. The patched version adds a byte counter limit of 0x1e1 (481) reads.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: The sponge must produce a long run of skip-decoding nibbles: for eta selector 4 (byte [r14+0x16]==4) nibbles >= 9, for eta selector 2 (byte [r14+0x16]==2) nibbles >= 15, decode to the 0x80 skip. Because the byte source is the Keccak sponge (not external signature data), this requires a pathological/corrupted sponge state rather than crafted input.
security_findings.primitive: Non-termination hardening — a defensive iteration bound on an ML-DSA rejection loop. No OOB read (byte source is a sponge, not a bounded buffer).
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly @ 0x18002A06C:
; Loop squeezes sponge bytes with no byte counter
000000018002A0EB  loc_18002A0EB:              ; main loop head
000000018002A0FC  call    SymCryptKeccakExtract ; squeeze 1 byte
000000018002A101  mov     cl, [rsp+140h+var_11F]
; ... nibble decoding, 0x80 skip marker does not advance output ...
000000018002A1A1  cmp     dl, 80h
000000018002A1A4  jz      short loc_18002A1C6 ; skip marker
000000018002A1CC  jb      loc_18002A0EB       ; cmp edi,100h/jb - only output count bounded, NO byte counter
000000018002A1D2  loc_18002A1D2:              ; exit when output full (edi >= 0x100)

; PATCHED SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly @ 0x180027128:
00000001800271A6  loc_1800271A6:              ; main loop head
00000001800271A6  cmp     esi, 1E1h          ; ADDED: sponge-byte limit (481), before squeeze
00000001800271AC  jnb     loc_1800272A9      ; ADDED: overflow -> error path
00000001800271C3  call    SymCryptKeccakExtract ; squeeze 1 byte
000000018002724F  inc     esi                ; ADDED: advance sponge-byte counter
000000018002729B  cmp     ebx, 100h
00000001800272A1  jb      loc_1800271A6      ; loop back; re-checks esi limit at head
00000001800272A9  mov     edi, 8008h         ; ADDED: SYMCRYPT_EXTERNAL_FAILURE

breakpoints.why: Break at function entry. This is SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly (secret-coefficient sampling). In the unpatched version, the inner byte-reading loop at 0x18002a0eb has no limit on sponge bytes consumed. Watch edi (coefficient counter) vs how many bytes are squeezed via SymCryptKeccakExtract (sub_18002bb78).

watch: edi = accepted coefficient counter, bounded by 0x100
byte [r14+0x16] = eta selector (2 or 4) — determines the nibble range
byte [rsp+0x21] = current squeezed sponge byte being decoded
r9 (arg4) = output buffer for sampled coefficients (0x100 entries max)
key_offsets: 0x18002A0FC: call to SymCryptKeccakExtract (sub_18002bb78) to squeeze next byte
0x18002A11A: nibble decoding for eta selector 2 (imul r8d, eax, 0Dh ...)
0x18002A140: nibble decoding for eta selector 4 (mov r8d, ebx; sub r8b, dl)
0x18002A1A1: cmp dl, 80h skip check (does not advance output)
0x18002A1CC: cmp edi, 100h / jb loc_18002A0EB — loop-back, no sponge-byte counter
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Requires a sponge state producing a long run of skip-decoding nibbles: for eta selector 4, nibble values >= 9; for eta selector 2, nibble values >= 15. 2. Reach the function via ML-DSA secret sampling (SymCryptMlDsaExpandS). 3. The loop will squeeze many bytes without advancing the coefficient counter.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In a debugger, the instruction pointer stuck in the loop at 0x18002a0eb-0x18002a1d2, with edi not advancing (below 0x100) while SymCryptKeccakExtract is called repeatedly. No OOB read (bytes come from the sponge, not a bounded buffer). The patched build exits with 0x8008 after 0x1e1 bytes.
debugging_aids.notes: The nibble mapping uses ((nibble * 0xd) >> 6) * 5 - nibble + 2. For nibble >= 9 (eta selector 4) or >= 15 (eta selector 2), the result is out of range and set to 0x80 (skip marker). Bytes come from the SHAKE/Keccak sponge seeded during ML-DSA key expansion, not from a certificate field.

security_findings.entry_point: Code Integrity SI-policy initialization / driver-signing-level evaluation (SIPolicyInitSystem -> CipInitializeSiPolicy); result sets the custom-kernel-signers bit (bit 0xD) in g_CiDeveloperMode
security_findings.vulnerability_class: CWE-693 - protection mechanism failure (kernel-mode driver signature enforcement / custom kernel signers)
security_findings.description: SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed decides whether a Code Integrity driver-signing evaluation may accept custom (non-Microsoft) kernel signers by inspecting the enable flags at offset +0x708 of each loaded SI policy. The unpatched version (@0x1800706C4) walks the entire g_SiPolicyHandles array and returns true as soon as ANY policy has the bit set, with no lock/enable gate and no distinction between base and supplemental policies - so a supplemental SI policy could enable custom kernel signers. The patched version (@0x1800764A8) adds two restrictions: (1) it returns false unless the new byte cs:byte_180048F08 has been explicitly set (checked at 0x1800764BD, and again inside the new SIPolicyCheckCustomKernelSignersAllowed @0x18007652C), written only by the new SIPolicySetCustomKernelSignersLockedEnabled @0x180076558 from a registry-backed init path (call @0x180018EAD); and (2) it calls SIPolicyIsBasePolicy on each handle and stops on any non-base policy, so supplemental policies are excluded. The change is strictly stricter and sits on the kernel-mode driver-signature-enforcement trust boundary. It is not part of the statically-linked SymCrypt library and is not gated by any WIL/Feature_* staging flag.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Load a supplemental SI policy that sets the custom-kernel-signers enable bit at policy field +0x708, then reach the CI SI-policy initialization / driver-signing evaluation path where SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed runs (unpatched caller @0x18001A4D8, result folded into g_CiDeveloperMode bit 0xD). In the unpatched build the supplemental policy alone causes the function to return true (custom kernel signers enabled); in the patched build the lock byte byte_180048F08 must be set and only a base policy is honored, so the supplemental policy has no effect. Practical abuse requires the privilege to install/load a supplemental SI policy.
security_findings.primitive: Protection-mechanism weakening on the kernel-mode driver-signature-enforcement trust boundary: pre-patch, a lower-trust supplemental SI policy could enable acceptance of custom/non-Microsoft kernel signers (custom-kernel-signers bit in g_CiDeveloperMode). Not a memory-corruption primitive.
security_findings.evidence:
// UNPATCHED SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed @ 0x1800706C4 - any policy, no lock, no base filter:
00000001800706CA  cmp     r9d, cs:g_NumberOfSiPolicies
00000001800706E1  mov     eax, [rax+708h]
00000001800706EB  test    al, 18h
00000001800706EF  test    al, 4
00000001800706F8  mov     r8b, 1

// PATCHED SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed @ 0x1800764A8 - lock gate + base-policy filter:
00000001800764BD  cmp     cs:byte_180048F08, r9b
00000001800764C4  jnz     short loc_1800764CA
00000001800764C6  xor     al, al
00000001800764E4  call    SIPolicyIsBasePolicy
00000001800764EB  jz      short loc_180076510
00000001800764F6  call    SIPolicyCheckCustomKernelSignersAllowed
security_findings.assembly:
; NEW SIPolicyCheckCustomKernelSignersAllowed @ 0x18007652C (re-checks the lock byte before the +0x708 flag test)
000000018007652C  cmp     cs:byte_180048F08, 0
0000000180076533  jz      short loc_18007654C
0000000180076535  test    dl, dl
0000000180076537  jz      short loc_18007654C
0000000180076539  test    cl, 8
000000018007654C  xor     al, al
000000018007654E  retn
; NEW SIPolicySetCustomKernelSignersLockedEnabled @ 0x180076558 (only writer of the lock byte)
0000000180076558  mov     cs:byte_180048F08, cl
000000018007655E  retn
; registry-backed writer of the lock value
0000000180018E9F  call    cs:__imp_ZwSetValueKey
0000000180018EAD  call    SIPolicySetCustomKernelSignersLockedEnabled
security_findings.debugging_aids: byte_180048F08 has zero references in ci_unpatched.dll and three in ci_patched.dll (cmp @0x1800764BD, cmp @0x18007652C, mov @0x180076558). SIPolicyCheckCustomKernelSignersAllowed and SIPolicySetCustomKernelSignersLockedEnabled have no content match in the unpatched binary. SIPolicyIsBasePolicy already exists unpatched (@0x1800713D8); what is new is that the custom-kernel-signers decision now consults it - the unpatched SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed (@0x1800706C4) never called it. Callers of SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed: unpatched @0x18001A4D8; patched @0x18001A7AC and @0x18006BC0C.

summary: The changed functions are all part of the statically-linked SymCrypt cryptographic library inside Windows Code Integrity (ci.dll), not the Authenticode/PE certificate-chain code. The patch hardens SymCrypt's FIPS 204 ML-DSA (post-quantum) sampling routines and the RSA private-key setup path. In SymCryptMlDsaExpandS (sub_180029484) the return value of the per-polynomial sampler SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly (sub_18002a06c) was discarded and the function always returned 0 (CWE-252/CWE-754); the patch propagates the error. SymCryptRsakeyCalculatePrivateFields (sub_180002d3c) gains an RSA key-consistency check that verifies the two prime factors multiply back to the modulus, returning 0x800e (SYMCRYPT_INVALID_ARGUMENT) on mismatch (CWE-754). Two ML-DSA rejection-sampling loops, SymCryptMlDsaSampleInBall (sub_18002a2e8) and SymCryptMlDsaRejBoundedPoly (sub_18002a06c), gain finite iteration bounds (0x79 and 0x1e1) returning 0x8008 (SYMCRYPT_EXTERNAL_FAILURE) on overflow (CWE-835); both draw bytes from a SHAKE/Keccak sponge, so the pre-patch risk is non-termination under a corrupted sponge state rather than an attacker-crafted signature, and there is no OOB read. These are robustness/hardening fixes in key-generation, key-import, and self-test crypto code; they are not a PE/driver code-signing bypass. Separately from SymCrypt, the patch also hardens the Code Integrity SI-policy custom-kernel-signers decision (Finding 5): SIPolicyAreCustomKernelSignersAllowed (@0x1800706C4 -> @0x1800764A8) previously accepted the enable bit at policy field +0x708 from any loaded SI policy, including supplemental ones, with no lock gate; the patched version requires the new lock/enable byte byte_180048F08 to be set (written by the new SIPolicySetCustomKernelSignersLockedEnabled @0x180076558 from a registry-backed init path) and restricts the enabling policy to a base policy via SIPolicyIsBasePolicy. This is a protection-mechanism-failure fix (CWE-693, medium severity) on the kernel-mode driver-signature-enforcement trust boundary, reachable during SI-policy init where the result feeds g_CiDeveloperMode bit 0xD.
